hero.title:
Why
Vichada?
hero.subtitle: This project is not random. It is a chosen location with unique geographical features, legal conditions and future advantages to build a planned community without conflict with existing society.

territory.title:
15 Reasons
for Vichada
territory.subtitle: Every detail has been analyzed. Every decision has a strategic foundation.

01.title: Size + demographic emptiness
01.subtitle: Size + demographic emptiness
01.body: ~100,000 km² with ~80,000 inhabitants. Very low density — room to plan without social friction.

02.title: Flat savannah
02.subtitle: Flat savannah — cheap engineering
02.body: Almost flat topography — roads, water pipes, energy and urbanism much cheaper than in mountains. No deforestation for expansion.

03.title: Strategic water
03.subtitle: Strategic water — Orinoco basin
03.body: In a century of water stress, this is worth gold. Rivers, streams, aquifers and stable rainfall.

04.title: Legal framework: ZIDRES
04.subtitle: Legal framework built for this
04.body: The State has already declared that it wants organized projects there. Not going against the law — going with the law.

05.title: Distance from urban poles
05.subtitle: Distance from urban poles
05.body: Less criminal pressure. Less cultural shock. Less 'chaotic pull' typical of big cities.

06.title: Live border with Venezuela
06.subtitle: Live border with Venezuela
06.body: Geopolitical key for the future. Potential for a two-way legal and economic corridor.

07.title: Cheap and abundant land
07.subtitle: Cheap and abundant land
07.body: Can legally buy large extensions. Scalable for decades.

08.title: Open sky for solar energy
08.subtitle: Open sky for solar energy
08.body: High radiation, few shadows, perfect terrain for solar farms.

09.title: Almost no urban legacy
09.subtitle: Almost no urban legacy
09.body: No previous informal settlements. No old designs that limit the plan.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Vichada was not chosen to isolate itself, but because it is the only place where it is possible to build an organized, lawful and future community without conflicting with existing society, while bringing development, investment and human capital to a forgotten region.

growth.title:
Build for
decades.
growth.subtitle: Future
growth.description: This is not a 5-year project. This is a territory that allows growing for 50-100 years without physical limits.

colombia.subtitle: Strategic value
colombia.description: And Vichada is its empty border.

items.noDisasters: Free from hurricanes, earthquakes and wars
items.gateway: Gateway between Amazon, Andes and Orinoco

home.subtitle: We are building the world's first esperanto community, starting with the founding community in Vichada. As a participant, you can collaborate in any community of our global network.

manifesto.title: MANIFESTO OF ESPERANTISTUJO
manifesto.subtitle: From Tribalism to Humanism
manifesto.tagline: Global Network of Communities • One Planet • One Common Language

sections.title: THE PROBLEM: THE INHERITED MINDSET OF THE NATION-STATE
sections.content:
The current world still operates according to the same mental model that tribes had thousands of years ago. We only changed the name: from tribalism to nationalism. And with that new name we added something more difficult: borders.

Everything between these borders belongs to one state; what is outside them, to another. And as in every tribe, conflicts become almost inevitable.

This model has proven inadequate for today's humanity:

1. **Corruption and inefficiency** in opaque bureaucracies
2. **Human capital flight**, because talent remains trapped in birthplace
3. **Chronic insecurity** in forgotten regions
4. **Monetary systems**, which often weaken the savings of ordinary people

Thinking from the macro (states) to the micro (people) generates division.
We propose the opposite: **thinking from people to the community**.

sections.title: OUR VISION: GLOBAL NETWORK OF ESPERANTO COMMUNITIES
sections.content:
We believe that all people are equally valuable, regardless of nationality, origin or religion.

We imagine a world where people can collaborate beyond borders through a common language and shared values.

Therefore we propose the creation of **physical communities**, connected in a **global network**, where Esperanto is the everyday language of coexistence.

The first such community will be founded in Vichada, Colombia, as a model for future communities in other parts of the world.

sections.title: TECHNOLOGICAL AND ORGANIZATIONAL PILLARS
sections.content:
1. **Ágora – Coordination System**
   Open-source tool to propose, finance and realize projects in the community with full transparency.

2. **Common Reputation System**
   Tool to facilitate trade between communities of the network, managed locally according to community principles.

3. **Task-based Collaboration**
   Roles are not permanent positions, but temporary tasks according to abilities and proven contributions.

4. **Digital Trust System**
   The contributions of each member are visible and traceable throughout the entire network of communities.

sections.title: HOW IT WORKS IN PRACTICE
sections.content:
**First phase: the founding community** in Vichada.

**Second phase:** help other groups found similar communities in other regions.

**Third phase:** connect these communities in a global collaboration network.

Security, economy and organization are based on transparency, local responsibility and mutual trust.

sections.title: CALL TO ACTION
sections.content:
We do not oppose existing states.
We simply build an example of another way of living together.

1. Join as a **participant** and contribute your abilities.
2. Help create the first esperanto community where the language is an everyday reality.

This is a practical experiment:
what happens when a community organizes around transparency, contribution and a common language?

Humanity doesn't need more borders.
It needs more collaborative nodes.

position.title: Our position
position.subtitle: We are not utopians. We are builders.
position.zamenhofText1: In his later years, Zamenhof no longer spoke only about language, but about
position.zamenhofText2: humanitarianism: the idea that the real problem of humanity is not lack of communication tools, but the way people divide into separate tribes called nations.
position.zamenhofText3: In his private letters, he expressed concern that an international language, if it remains only a tool for congresses and correspondence, risks becoming an intellectual circle instead of a living reality. For him, the language had to be
position.zamenhofText4: spoken daily among people of different origins.
position.zamenhofText5: His vision was not just to communicate without borders, but that people actually
position.zamenhofText6: live together without borders. That they live, work and build together, so that the language becomes a natural part of daily life.
position.zamenhofText7: Esperantistujo is not a new idea. It is an attempt to realize that unfulfilled part of Zamenhof's vision: to create places where Esperanto is not only spoken, but lived.

cta.title: Become a participant
cta.description: Register as a participant of the world's first esperanto community. Start in the founding community in Vichada, Colombia, and collaborate in any community of our global network as we grow. The project hopes to finance your travel and living expenses in this pioneering city.
